paper_title: Exploring the effects of waste plastic aggregate on styrene-butadiene-styrenemodified asphalt binders for sustainable rural pavements

entities.description: The research focus on developing durable and cost-effective road surfaces for rural areas with limited resources.
The environmental concern of processing and repurposing massive amounts of annual plastic waste.
Recycled plastic particles (polyethylene, polypropylene, polystyrene) used as a partial replacement in asphalt mixtures.
A polymer modifier used to enhance the elasticity and fatigue resistance of asphalt binders.
A system for proportioning asphalt mixtures and compacting samples using a gyratory compactor.
A test to evaluate the tensile strength and cracking resistance of asphalt specimens.
A test used to evaluate the rutting resistance of asphalt mixtures under simulated traffic loading.
A test to assess stiffness and deformation resistance across different loading frequencies.
A test used to determine the moisture susceptibility of asphalt mixtures.
The highest tensile strength ratio observed in the mixture without WPA.
The tensile strength ratio observed at the highest concentration of waste plastic aggregate.
A measure of the temperature at which the SBS-modified asphalt binder begins to soften.
The indirect tensile strength value for the 6% waste plastic content mixture.
The stiffness value recorded for the 6% waste plastic content mixture.
The final rutting depth measured after 20,000 cycles in the HWTT for the 6% WPA mixture.
The branch of engineering dealing with the design and construction of infrastructure like pavements.
Researcher from the Faculty of Civil Engineering, Can Tho University.
Researcher from the Faculty of Civil Engineering, Induk University.
Corresponding author from the Faculty of Civil Engineering, Nguyen Tat Thanh University.
Academic institution located in Viet Nam.
